description_long: This object is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a smooth, polished finish and visible wood grain. The handle is ergonomically shaped for a comfortable grip, tapering slightly towards the end. The axe head is made of metal, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade that has a straight cutting edge and a flat poll opposite the blade. The blade is securely mounted onto the handle, with the metal head enveloping the top portion of the wood. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for chopping wood or similar tasks, and the materials and construction suggest durability and reliability. No visible markings or decorative elements are present on either the handle or the blade.
description: A hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, wedge-shaped metal blade, designed for chopping tasks. The handle is smooth and ergonomic, while the blade is robust and functional.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad, flat metal blade.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the ergonomic curvature of the handle and the thickness of the blade.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's curve and the flat poll of the axe head.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the blade's edge profile and the end of the wooden handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a smooth, polished finish, ergonomic grip, and oval cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, wedge-shaped steel blade with a straight cutting edge and flat poll; single-edged with no beard.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted over the top of the wooden handle.
